title.purpose: Page and section headings across rooms, cards, and the dashboard.

metric.purpose: Dashboard status-count numbers only; tabular numerals.

body.purpose: Default UI text: table cells, form labels, buttons, nav.

label.purpose: Table column headers, status-pill text, field captions.

mono.purpose: The 5-digit keycard code, everywhere it appears.

shadows.name: whisper-lift
shadows.purpose: Hover-only elevation for clickable rows and card links. Never present at rest.

motion.name: ease-standard
motion.purpose: Default easing for hover/focus state transitions (150-200ms).

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The one filled action per screen: save, update status.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: #003F23; color: #FFFFFF; font: 500 0.875rem/1.5 'Geist Sans', ui-sans-serif, system-ui; padding: 8px 16px; border: none; border-radius: 6px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 180ms cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #002F15; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #003F23; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { background: #FFFFFF; color: #101814; font: 500 0.875rem/1.5 'Geist Sans', ui-sans-serif, system-ui; padding: 8px 16px; border: 1px solid #BFC6C2; border-radius: 6px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 180ms cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1); } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { background: #F0F5F2; } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #003F23; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Destructive Button
components.description: Delete room / delete card. Never filled solid so it never competes with the Primary button.
components.css: .ds-btn-destructive { background: #FFFFFF; color: #8A0314; font: 500 0.875rem/1.5 'Geist Sans', ui-sans-serif, system-ui; padding: 8px 16px; border: 1px solid #BFC6C2; border-radius: 6px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 180ms cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1); } .ds-btn-destructive:hover { background: #FFE4E1; border-color: #F09188; } .ds-btn-destructive:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #8A0314; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Status Pill — Active
components.description: Direct render of Keycard.status. One pill per card, tinted background with dark text, never a solid saturated fill.
components.css: .ds-status-pill { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; font: 500 0.75rem/1.4 'Geist Sans', ui-sans-serif, system-ui; padding: 2px 10px; border-radius: 9999px; } .ds-status-pill--active { background: #DEF1E0; color: #004010; }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Room number, keycard code, and other form fields. Bottle Green focus ring, no glow.
components.css: .ds-input { background: #FFFFFF; color: #101814; font: 400 0.875rem/1.5 'Geist Sans', ui-sans-serif, system-ui; padding: 8px 12px; border: 1px solid #BFC6C2; border-radius: 6px; width: 100%; box-sizing: border-box; transition: border-color 150ms cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1); } .ds-input:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #003F23; outline-offset: 1px; border-color: #003F23; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: #595F5C; }

components.name: Table Row (clickable)
components.description: A room or keycard row in a list table. Flat at rest; Whisper Lift shadow only on hover, signaling it leads somewhere on click.
components.css: .ds-row { display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: space-between; background: #FFFFFF; padding: 12px 16px; border-bottom: 1px solid #DBDFDC; cursor: pointer; transition: box-shadow 180ms cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1); } .ds-row:hover { box-shadow: 0 2px 10px oklch(0.32 0.085 160 / 0.10); } .ds-row__code { font: 500 0.875rem/1.5 'Geist Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; color: #101814; } .ds-row__meta { font: 400 0.875rem/1.5 'Geist Sans', ui-sans-serif, system-ui; color: #595F5C; }

narrative.overview: Keycard Record should feel like the front desk of a well-run boutique property with a vault quietly humming in the back office: unmistakably premium in its restraint, precise in its record-keeping, and calm under the weight of responsibility. Bottle-green ink and brass accents carry that feeling; everything else stays out of the way so the record itself reads as trustworthy. This spec establishes the brand color system for a codebase that, as of this writing, is entirely neutral (Tailwind's default zinc gray, no accent color, default green/yellow/red badge tints). Primary (Bottle Green) and Secondary (Brass) are new tokens to roll out across primary actions, navigation, and moments of emphasis.
keyCharacteristics: One committed brand color (Bottle Green) carries primary actions and identity; Brass is reserved for rare, deliberate emphasis.
Status (Active / Process Inactive / Inactive) has its own dedicated color family, kept visually distinct from Bottle Green and Brass.
Flat by default; a whisper-soft shadow appears only as a hover response on interactive rows and cards.
Single sans family (Geist) for everything except keycard codes, which run in Geist Mono for scanability.

rules.name: The Two-Color Rule
rules.body: Bottle Green and Brass are the only two colors that carry brand meaning. If a third "brand" color shows up anywhere, it's a mistake.

rules.name: The Status Never Borrows Rule
rules.body: Status pills never use Bottle Green or Brass, even when a status color happens to look similar, keeping system state visually separate from brand chrome.

rules.name: The One Face Rule
rules.body: No second display typeface. A heading that needs more weight gets a heavier cut of Geist, not a different family.

rules.name: The Flat-at-Rest Rule
rules.body: If it isn't being hovered or focused, it has no shadow. Depth is earned by interaction, not decoration.

dos: Do keep Paper (#FFFFFF) as the only background color at the page level — no cream, no sand, no warm-tinted neutral.
Do use Ink-colored text on any Brass fill, never white (white-on-Brass fails contrast at 2.33:1; Ink-on-Brass reaches 7.74:1).
Do keep status pills in their own dedicated hue family, distinct from Bottle Green and Brass.
Do reserve the filled Bottle Green button for exactly one primary action per screen.
Do run keycard codes in Geist Mono everywhere they appear, including inside form inputs.
donts: Don't introduce a third "brand" color. Two (Bottle Green, Brass) is the system.
Don't add a shadow to anything at rest — Whisper Lift only appears on hover.
Don't fall back to the generic all-gray/zinc admin-template look this spec replaces.
Don't use border-left or border-right as a colored accent stripe on cards, rows, or callouts.
Don't fill a status pill with a solid saturated color; it stays a tinted-background pill with dark text.
Don't reach for a second display typeface for emphasis — a heavier weight of Geist does the job.
